title: dalbergaria guttata
intro: Dalbergaria guttata, also known as the African Bloodroot or Sanguin, is a plant native to tropical Africa that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its root, which contains sanguinarine, a toxic alkaloid with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dalbergaria guttata include its use as an antiseptic to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include tinctures, decoctions, and ointments, which are used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
